channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Public skepticism grows as AI and crypto super PACs pour money into the 2026 midterms
item.description: A new POLITICO poll shows that Americans remain wary of both artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency even as industry-backed super PACs pour huge sums into the 2026 midterm elections. The article s...

item.title: Sydney Sweeney enjoys AFL match at SCG with Swans
item.description: American actress Sydney Sweeney, known for her role in Euphoria, was seen at the Sydney Cricket Ground (SCG) on Sunday afternoon cheering on the Sydney Swans during their home clash against Melbourne....

item.title: OPEC+ Raises Oil Quotas Amid UAE Exit, Focus on Market Stability
item.description: On Sunday, seven OPEC+ countries, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, don increase their oil production quotas for June by 188,000 barrels per day. This move na to show say the cartel still dey steady ...

item.title: Junior Boks secure U20 Rugby Championship title with dominant win over Australia
item.description: The Junior Springboks delivered a commanding performance in Gqeberha, beating Australia 56-17 to clinch the U20 Rugby Championship with a game still to play. Their bonus-point victory ensured an unass...

item.title: An Inside Look at the Sovereign Citizen Movement and Their Views on COVID-19 and Taxes
item.description: The sovereign citizen movement in Australia is gaining attention for its rejection of government authority, particularly in relation to COVID-19 and taxes. Members of the Sovereign People’s Assembly o...

item.title: Israel Confirms Purchase of Fighter Jets from the United States
item.description: Israel has officially approved a major defense agreement to acquire advanced fighter jets from the United States. The deal, endorsed by Israeli officials, marks a significant step in strengthening the...

item.title: Star-studded closing ceremony kicks off PSL 11 final at Gaddafi Stadium
item.description: The closing ceremony of the 11th edition of the HBL Pakistan Super League (PSL) was held in Lahore at the iconic Gaddafi Stadium, setting the stage for the tournament’s final match. The event attracte...

item.title: APC Accuses Peter Obi of Leaving Parties in Search of Uncontested Presidential Ticket
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don yan say former Anambra State governor, Peter Obi, don dey waka dey find one free and unchallenged presidential ticket. Dis na after Obi announce say he don como...

item.title: Iran indicates it will retain control over Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions with the US
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, has indicated that the country intends to maintain control over the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route through which r...

item.title: Joshua Garcia opens up about love and personal boundaries
item.description: In a recent interview, Joshua Garcia shared his thoughts on love, relationships, and the importance of setting personal boundaries. The young actor emphasized how vital it is to stay true to oneself w...

item.title: The God Christians Fail to Love and Appreciate, By Femi Aribisala
item.description: Femi Aribisala’s article continues to delve into the complex relationship between Christians and their understanding of God's goodness. He questions whether Christians truly appreciate God’s goodness,...

item.title: Danish official data show higher crime conviction rates among immigrants and descendants
item.description: The Daily Mail report discusses Danish government figures indicating that immigrants and their descendants have higher age-adjusted conviction rates than native Danes, with Lebanese-born men and those...

item.title: Overloaded Truck Causes Partial Collapse of Kaliwet Bailey Bridge in Ilocos Sur
item.description: A section of the Kaliwet Bailey bridge in Santo Domingo, Ilocos Sur collapsed on Saturday after an overloaded 10-wheeler truck crossed the structure. The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH)...

item.title: Impact of Canadian Boycott on U.S. Trade and Jobs Is Noticeable but Limited
item.description: Over the past year, some Canadians have deliberately reduced their purchases of U.S. goods and avoided travelling to the United States, creating a noticeable, though not overwhelming, economic impact....

item.title: Urgent action needed to curb crime and violence in South Africa
item.description: Narend Ganesh, a concerned citizen from Durban North, expresses his frustration over the rising tide of violent crimes in South Africa, including the recent Newark massacre where seven family members ...

item.title: BJP Slowly Increasing Political Presence in Kerala Amid LDF-UDF Dominance
item.description: As Kerala waits for the 2026 assembly election results, attention is shifting from whether BJP can form the government to how it may be reshaping the state's long-standing bipolar political landscape....

item.title: Rising health and interest costs limit Victoria’s budget flexibility ahead of state budget
item.description: Victoria’s state finances are under mounting pressure, with new analysis showing the government has limited room to manoeuvre as spending continues to outpace expectations. According to the e61 Instit...

item.title: Peter Obi and Kwankwaso Join NDC, Set to Meet Party Leaders
item.description: Peter Obi, former governor of Anambra State and 2023 Labour Party presidential candidate, alongside Musa Kwankwaso, former governor of Kano State and ex-presidential candidate of the NNPP, don land th...

item.title: Parliament Committee and USAf Discuss Role of Foreign Academics in South African Universities
item.description: The Portfolio Committee on Higher Education and Training, chaired by Tebogo Letsie, has raised concerns about the employment of foreign academics at South African universities. Letsie described recent...

item.title: Understanding the Use and Benefits of Black Timber Cladding in UK Architecture
item.description: Black timber cladding, particularly charred timber inspired by the Japanese Shou Sugi Ban technique, has become increasingly popular in contemporary UK architecture. Unlike painted or coated timber, c...
